pattern: Visiting a BST's keys in descending order with a reverse in-order walk lets a running suffix sum convert each key into itself plus everything larger.
hints: An in-order walk of a BST yields keys in increasing order, so reversing the traversal direction yields them decreasing.
If you process keys from largest to smallest while keeping a cumulative sum, that sum already covers every key greater than the current one.
Recurse right first, then add the node's value to an accumulator and store the accumulator back into the node, then recurse left.

edge_inputs.description: root with only a larger right child

stress.description: random BST built by inserting distinct shuffled keys, serialized level-order
stress.note: distinct keys preserve the BST property
